impact:
PARTIALLY FIXED by ISS-008 (2026-07-16). Blocker RE-POINTED from ISS-009 to ISS-013 — read this before re-measuring.

*** THE ORIGINAL DIAGNOSIS WAS WRONG AND IS RETRACTED. *** This was recorded as "+6 dB at BL=0.65 is
NodalCircuit impedance loading — NOT fixable by a scalar". It was, in fact, exactly a scalar:
kDryGain[1] = 2.534 multiplied V1L's dry/wet ratio by +8.1 dB (a per-path fudge applied only to the
dry leg — see ISS-008 for why that is unphysical). Removing it moved the V1L partial-blend captures:
    BL=0.65: raw clean null -9.6 -> -12.7 dB
    BL=0.30: raw clean null -1.9 ->  -4.1 dB
while both V1L BL=1.00 captures were unchanged — the signature of a dry-leg-only error. The retracted
explanation predicted the opposite (a scalar could not fix it); do not resurrect it without new
evidence. kDryGain NO LONGER EXISTS and must not be reintroduced; the dry/wet ratio is set by the
circuit alone and is not a fit target.

WHAT REMAINS. V1L BL=0.65 FR rms is still 7.45 dB and BL=0.30 is 9.55 dB, so a real residual survives.

BLOCKER RE-POINTED (twice, same day): ISS-009 -> ISS-013 -> **ISS-001**. ISS-013 was closed as INVALID (its 'plugin over-rolls LF with drive' premise was a peak-referenced-metric + 25 Hz-noise artifact; the plugin's LF is drive-independent within 2.24 dB at 40-100 Hz). The real wet-path LF contamination is the PEDAL compressing at LF while the plugin under-clips = the clip-onset gap tracked by ISS-001. The old reason was "this is measured against a V1L wet path
that is -12.9 dB LF-deficient (ISS-009's C10)". ISS-009 is now closed: C10 is EXONERATED (schematic
re-crop confirms 10n/100k; the wet path is +2.8 dB of its §1 target at §1 conditions). The wet-path LF contamination is real, but its cause is ISS-001's clip-onset gap, NOT a linear filter error. The blocking logic is unchanged in substance: BLEND still mixes dry against a wet path that
is wrong at LF whenever DRIVE is up, and these V1L blend captures are at D0.45/D0.40, so they carry it.
cascade_analysis.py section C's "adding dry partially CANCELS the wet path's LF deficit" reading still
stands.

Next: land ISS-001's clip-onset fix, then re-measure this from scratch. With kDryGain gone AND clip onset
correct, the remaining residual may be small enough to close outright.
title: V1L blend residual at partial BLEND settings
